curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 10 units, 9 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify different types of solutions based on the state of solvent and solute
Express concentration using various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction, etc.)
Explain factors affecting solubility of solids and gases in liquids
Apply Raoult's Law to ideal liquid solutions and deviations in non-ideal solutions
Calculate molar mass using colligative properties such as relative lowering of vapor pressure, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure
Analyze causes for abnormal molecular masses observed through colligative property measurements
Differentiate between galvanic (voltaic) and electrolytic cells
Explain the working principle of electrochemical cells including electrode reactions
Apply Nernst equation to calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions
Relate conductivity, molar conductivity, and degree of dissociation in electrolytic solutions
Describe the principles behind batteries (primary/secondary) and fuel cells
Explain the mechanism of corrosion and methods for its prevention

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment. Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in the previous step
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment involving dialysis of sols.

practicals.title: Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment studying emulsions and emulsifying agents.

practicals.title: Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment measuring reaction rates under varying conditions.

practicals.title: Study of reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide OR Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate (clock reaction)
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment. Select one option to study reaction kinetics.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of dissolution or neutralization OR Enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
practicals.details: Thermochemistry experiment. Select one option to determine enthalpy changes.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes
practicals.details: Electrochemistry experiment studying the effect of electrolyte concentration on cell potential.

practicals.title: Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment involving plant extracts.

practicals.title: Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only by paper chromatography
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment for separating cations with large difference in Rf values.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation experiment.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds: Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
practicals.details: Organic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
practicals.details: Qualitative analysis of organic compounds to identify specific functional groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
practicals.details: Analysis of biological macromolecules in both pure forms and food items.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis. Students must prepare standard solutions themselves.

practicals.title: Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Inorganic qualitative analysis covering specified lists of cations (Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+) and anions.

practicals.title: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus
practicals.details: Assessment of familiarity with laboratory equipment including beakers, burettes, pipettes, Bunsen burners, etc., and hands-on skills like odour detection.

practicals.title: Written test on practicals
practicals.details: 30-minute written examination containing 15 skill-based questions for visually challenged learners (answer any 10).

projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption, colloidal state distinction, lyophilic/lyophobic properties, coagulation, emulsion types.
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements - Extraction principles, concentration, oxidation/reduction methods.
Polymers – Polymerisation, Homopolymers and copolymers.
Chemistry in Everyday life - Chemicals in medicines (analgesics, tranquilizers, etc.), chemicals in food (preservatives, sweeteners).
internal_assessment.note: Topics included for formative assessment only to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis: Preparation of standard solution (Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate) and determination of KMnO4 molarity.
Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt.
optional_experiments_section_a: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Study of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples.
Preparation and comparison of soybean milk with natural milk.
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions.
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (effect of pH/temperature).
Comparative study of fermentation rates (wheat flour, gram flour, etc.).
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi.
Study of common food adulterants.

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment. Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in the previous step
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment involving dialysis of sols.

practicals.title: Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment studying emulsions and emulsifying agents.

practicals.title: Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment measuring reaction rates under varying conditions.

practicals.title: Study of reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide OR Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate (clock reaction)
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment. Select one option to study reaction kinetics.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of dissolution or neutralization OR Enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
practicals.details: Thermochemistry experiment. Select one option to determine enthalpy changes.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes
practicals.details: Electrochemistry experiment studying the effect of electrolyte concentration on cell potential.

practicals.title: Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment involving plant extracts.

practicals.title: Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only by paper chromatography
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment for separating cations with large difference in Rf values.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation experiment.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds: Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
practicals.details: Organic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
practicals.details: Qualitative analysis of organic compounds to identify specific functional groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
practicals.details: Analysis of biological macromolecules in both pure forms and food items.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis. Students must prepare standard solutions themselves.

practicals.title: Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Inorganic qualitative analysis covering specified lists of cations (Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+) and anions.

practicals.title: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus
practicals.details: Assessment of familiarity with laboratory equipment including beakers, burettes, pipettes, Bunsen burners, etc., and hands-on skills like odour detection.

practicals.title: Written test on practicals
practicals.details: 30-minute written examination containing 15 skill-based questions for visually challenged learners (answer any 10).

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption, colloidal state distinction, lyophilic/lyophobic properties, coagulation, emulsion types.
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements - Extraction principles, concentration, oxidation/reduction methods.

Chemistry in Everyday life - Chemicals in medicines (analgesics, tranquilizers, etc.), chemicals in food (preservatives, sweeteners).
internal_assessment.note: Topics included for formative assessment only to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

competency_groups.title: Solutions
competency_groups.description: Understanding types of solutions, concentration expressions, solubility principles, vapor pressure behavior in ideal and non-ideal systems, colligative properties for molar mass determination, and identification of abnormal molecular masses.
competencies: Identify different types of solutions based on the state of solvent and solute
Express concentration using various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction, etc.)
Explain factors affecting solubility of solids and gases in liquids
Apply Raoult's Law to ideal liquid solutions and deviations in non-ideal solutions
Calculate molar mass using colligative properties such as relative lowering of vapor pressure, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure
Analyze causes for abnormal molecular masses observed through colligative property measurements

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry
competency_groups.description: Knowledge of electrochemical cells, galvanic cell operation, Nernst equation application, conductance in electrolytic solutions, electrolysis principles, battery and fuel cell mechanisms, and corrosion processes.
competencies: Differentiate between galvanic (voltaic) and electrolytic cells
Explain the working principle of electrochemical cells including electrode reactions
Apply Nernst equation to calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions
Relate conductivity, molar conductivity, and degree of dissociation in electrolytic solutions
Describe the principles behind batteries (primary/secondary) and fuel cells
Explain the mechanism of corrosion and methods for its prevention

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics
competency_groups.description: Understanding reaction rates, factors influencing them, integrated rate laws for zero and first-order reactions, temperature dependence via Arrhenius equation, and collision theory fundamentals.
competencies: Define rate of a chemical reaction and express it mathematically
Analyze the effect of concentration, temperature, catalysts, and surface area on reaction rates
Derive and apply integrated rate equations for zero-order and first-order reactions to determine half-life and rate constants
Explain the Arrhenius equation relating activation energy and temperature dependence of rate constant
Describe collision theory concepts including effective collisions and orientation factors

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements
competency_groups.description: Familiarity with periodic table position, electronic configurations of transition elements, general properties including variable oxidation states and magnetic behavior, important compounds of d-block elements, lanthanoids, actinoids, and their applications.
competencies: Locate d-block and f-block elements in the periodic table based on electron configuration
Explain electronic configurations and stability trends for transition metals
Correlate general properties (color, magnetism, catalytic activity) with incomplete d-orbitals
Identify important compounds of transition elements and their uses
Distinguish between lanthanoids and actinoids regarding oxidation states and radioactivity
List applications of d-block and f-block elements in industry and technology
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds
competency_groups.description: Understanding Werner's theory, nomenclature rules, isomerism types (structural and stereoisomerism), bonding theories including Valence Bond Theory and Crystal Field Theory, metal carbonyls, and applications.
competencies: Explain Werner's postulates regarding primary and secondary valency
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to write names of coordination compounds correctly
Identify different types of isomerism in coordination complexes (ionization, hydrate, linkage, geometrical, optical)
Predict geometry and magnetic properties using Valence Bond Theory and Crystal Field Theory
Describe the nature of bonding in metal carbonyls including synergic effect

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes
competency_groups.description: Classification, nomenclature of halogen derivatives, nature of C-X bond, preparation methods for haloalkanes and haloarenes, physical properties, chemical reactions including substitution and elimination mechanisms.
competencies: Classify alkyl and aryl halides based on the number of halogen atoms
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to name haloalkanes and haloarenes
Explain the nature of C-X bond polarity and its effect on reactivity
Describe methods for preparing haloalkanes from alcohols, hydrocarbons, etc.
Differentiate between SN1 and SN2 reaction mechanisms with examples

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers
competency_groups.description: Classification of alcohols, phenols, and ethers; nomenclature; structures of functional groups; preparation methods; physical properties including hydrogen bonding effects.
competencies: Classify alcohols as primary, secondary, or tertiary based on carbon attachment
Distinguish between alcohols and phenols structurally and chemically
Apply IUPAC nomenclature for polyhydric alcohols, phenols, and ethers
Explain the role of hydrogen bonding in physical properties like boiling point and solubility

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids
competency_groups.description: Nomenclature of carbonyl compounds; preparation methods for aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids; physical properties; characteristic chemical reactions including nucleophilic addition.
competencies: Differentiate between aldehydes and ketones based on structure
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to carbonyl compounds and carboxylic acids
Describe preparation methods for aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids from various precursors
Predict products of nucleophilic addition reactions involving carbonyl groups
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines
competency_groups.description: Structure of amines; classification into primary, secondary, tertiary; nomenclature; preparation methods including reduction and Gabriel phthalimide synthesis; physical properties and chemical reactivity.
competencies: Classify amines based on the number of alkyl/aryl groups attached to nitrogen
Apply IUPAC rules for naming aliphatic and aromatic amines
Describe preparation methods including reduction of nitro compounds, Hoffmann bromamide degradation, and Gabriel phthalimide synthesis
Explain basicity trends in amines compared to ammonia

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Classification and structure of carbohydrates, proteins; role of enzymes as biological catalysts; types and functions of vitamins; structure of nucleic acids (DNA/RNA); hormonal regulation.
competencies: Classify carbohydrates into monosaccharides, disaccharides, and polysaccharides
Explain the primary and secondary structures of proteins including peptide bonds
Describe the catalytic mechanism of enzymes in biological systems
Categorize vitamins as water-soluble or fat-soluble with examples

practicals.title: Preparation of one lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum) and one lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment. Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in the previous step
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment involving dialysis of sols.

practicals.title: Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion of different oils
practicals.details: Surface Chemistry experiment studying emulsions and emulsifying agents.

practicals.title: Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment measuring reaction rates under varying conditions.

practicals.title: Study of reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide OR Potassium Iodate and Sodium Sulphate (clock reaction)
practicals.details: Chemical Kinetics experiment. Select one option to study reaction kinetics.

practicals.title: Enthalpy of dissolution or neutralization OR Enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform
practicals.details: Thermochemistry experiment. Select one option to determine enthalpy changes.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn || Cu/Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes
practicals.details: Electrochemistry experiment studying the effect of electrolyte concentration on cell potential.

practicals.title: Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment involving plant extracts.

practicals.title: Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only by paper chromatography
practicals.details: Chromatography experiment for separating cations with large difference in Rf values.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
practicals.details: Inorganic compound preparation experiment.

practicals.title: Preparation of Organic Compounds: Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, or Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
practicals.details: Organic compound preparation. Select one option for synthesis.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups
practicals.details: Qualitative analysis of organic compounds to identify specific functional groups.

practicals.title: Characteristic tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples and their detection in given foodstuffs
practicals.details: Analysis of biological macromolecules in both pure forms and food items.

practicals.title: Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution by titration against Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate
practicals.details: Volumetric analysis. Students must prepare standard solutions themselves.

practicals.title: Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
practicals.details: Inorganic qualitative analysis covering specified lists of cations (Pb, Cu, Al, Fe, Mn, Ni, Zn, Co, Ca, Sr, Ba, Mg, NH4+) and anions.

practicals.title: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus
practicals.details: Assessment of familiarity with laboratory equipment including beakers, burettes, pipettes, Bunsen burners, etc., and hands-on skills like odour detection.

practicals.title: Written test on practicals
practicals.details: 30-minute written examination containing 15 skill-based questions for visually challenged learners (answer any 10).

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption, colloidal state distinction, lyophilic/lyophobic properties, coagulation, emulsion types.
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements - Extraction principles, concentration, oxidation/reduction methods.
Polymers – Polymerisation, Homopolymers and copolymers.
Chemistry in Everyday life - Chemicals in medicines (analgesics, tranquilizers, etc.), chemicals in food (preservatives, sweeteners).
internal_assessment.note: Topics included for formative assessment only to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

mandatory_experiments_section_b: Quantitative analysis: Preparation of standard solution (Oxalic acid or Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate) and determination of KMnO4 molarity.
Qualitative Analysis: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt.
optional_experiments_section_a: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents)

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples.

Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions.
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (effect of pH/temperature).
Comparative study of fermentation rates (wheat flour, gram flour, etc.).
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi.
Study of common food adulterants.
